title: Coleataenia condensa
intro: Coleataenia condensa, commonly known as the Guatemalan wild rice, is a perennial grass native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which offer potential benefits in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coleataenia condensa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as isopropyl amine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coleataenia condensa is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Coleataenia condensa is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae. More specifically, Coleataenia condensa falls under the tribe Paniceae. The classification further categorizes it as belonging to the tribe Paniceae and genus Coleataenia. This classification provides a clearer understanding of its evolutionary relationships and position within the plant kingdom.
